column50:
Dominique Farrar is a California-born, Paris-based wellness educator, experience curator, and the founder of Well in France, a company dedicated to crafting IRL experiences that make wellness feel joyful, personal, and actually doable.

Before building a life and business in France, Dom spent more than 15 years in the tech world leading marketing, community, and experiential strategy for global brands. She built and scaled communities at Yelp, developed founder and CEO communities at a leading Bay Area venture capital firm, and later expanded a CFO community from 2,000 to 10,000 members at the French fintech company Spendesk. Most recently, she built a global thought-leadership and strategy community during her time at Quantive. Her superpower has always been curating people, spaces, and experiences that spark connection long before she ever applied it to wellness.

Today, she brings that same community-led expertise into Well in France, where she designs retreats, intimate “Women & Wellness” dinners in Paris, and bespoke brand and influencer experiences for companies looking to create meaningful, human-centric touchpoints. Her work sits at the intersection of experiential marketing, community-building, and holistic wellbeing, rooted in the belief that in a noisy, digital-first world, taste, curation, and real-life experiences are a brand’s most powerful differentiators.

And yes, she was pretty much destined for France. Her mom, a Paris-obsessed flight attendant, gave her a very French name despite zero actual French roots in the family, and Dom eventually followed it to the real thing. A former dancer who still believes movement is medicine, she lives with her husband and kids in Paris, keeps lipstick stashed in every room “just in case,” and is on a lifelong mission to make wellness feel less like homework and more like a vibrant, lived experience you actually look forward to.

column32: In my world - scaling ventures for growth and exit - the company that wins is the one that finds the convergence of that technology, the right market timing, and a ruthless focus on commercial viability.  My work isn't just about backing the best tech. It's about aligning that deep tech with a clear path to market, positive unit economics, and a strategic exit. Building the 0-to-1 product is the technical challenge; scaling it from 1-to-N is the commercial one, and that's the part people most often get wrong.
column34: Avoid marketing hype and buzzwords.  Phrases like "revolutionary," "game-changing," "magic," or "unprecedented" tend to obscure what's actually happening. My whole approach is to be "hype-free" and provide pragmatic advice.  Also, there's a key difference between making a topic "relatable" (which I love to do, often with pop culture) and "dumbing it down" (which we should avoid). My audience is technical and appreciates diving deep beneath the surface-level commentary.

column39: At Sakura Sky, our work is built on three core pillars: Cloud, Data & AI, and Security.  Cloud is our foundation. We’re not just resellers; we’re hands-on architects who build the high-performance, secure, and scalable cloud environments that high-growth ventures need to run on.  Data & AI is where we're hyper-focused today. We bridge the gap between a company's raw data and its actual potential. This covers everything from data engineering and MLOps to building the governance frameworks that make AI usable and trustworthy in the enterprise.  Security is the non-negotiable philosophy that's woven through everything we do. We have a "Shift-Left" approach that embeds security and compliance into the architecture and development lifecycle from day one, not as an afterthought.  From those services, we've built four repeatable solutions to help our clients move faster:  First are our two "strategy and enablement" frameworks. Catalyst is our strategic roadmapping framework to align your data and AI goals with business outcomes. Accelerate is our hands-on sprint to take your team from zero to a working prototype in their own environment, fast.  Next are our two "infrastructure and governance" solutions. Enclave is our modular, pre-built cloud foundation. It’s our best-practice, secure-by-default environment built on Terraform that gets you from a blank slate to a fully governed, multi-project cloud organization.  And finally, Sentinel, which is our specialized security and compliance solution for the AI/ML lifecycle. It audits everything from the code and data pipelines to the models themselves, checking for vulnerabilities, data drift, and integrity. This is becoming absolutely critical for governing the new wave of autonomous AI agents. https://www.sakurasky.com/our-solutions/ and https://www.sakurasky.com/what-we-do/

column50:
Andrew Stevens is a technology leader who has spent three decades building, scaling, and selling companies across cloud, AI, cybersecurity, gaming, and media. As Co-Founder of Sakura Sky and CTO at Orbx, he leads global engineering teams that take ventures from the 0→1 build to the far trickier 1→N scale, where technical ambition meets commercial reality.

With roots in mathematical modeling and environmental prediction, Andrew built early internet infrastructure in Australia, created a consumer sports-tech product that reached more than half the Australian adult population, and went on to build and sell multiple companies to the likes of Disney, ESPN and AT&T. He later helped legacy giants like Lonely Planet and major news organizations reinvent themselves for the digital era. Today, he works with high-growth companies, investors, and founders to build resilient, governed, and secure AI and cloud systems that stand up under real-world pressure.

Andrew’s philosophy is simple: the ventures that win aren’t the ones with the flashiest tech; they’re the ones that find the convergence of timing, engineering rigor, market viability, and sustainable growth. He brings a builder’s mindset and a commercially-minded investor lens to every conversation, helping technical teams think more strategically and helping founders understand what “scale” actually requires under the hood.

Based between Europe and the US, Andrew has lived a few unexpected parallel lives: bass guitarist, Taekwondo black belt instructor, published photographer, and survivor of a life-altering motorcycle accident that forced him to rebuild from nothing. That perspective shapes the way he works: grounded, resilient, and unafraid to challenge convention. 

Known for making complex systems relatable without sanding off their sophistication, he blends deep technical fluency with pop-culture analogies, straight talk, and a firm rejection of buzzwords. Whether he’s breaking down AI guardrails, engineering culture, founder mindset, or scaling strategy, his through-line is constant: cut the noise, tell the truth, and build technology that actually works in the real world.

column49: Sunaina Mehta is Founder and Self-Leadership Director of Design Your Next, a virtual studio helping women create meaningful change and define success on their own terms. Her work is rooted in a belief she has lived by her entire life: choosing yourself is the most graceful act of rebellion — and the path to your own becoming. Born between America and India, Sunaina grew up navigating cultures, careers, and expectations, learning early that growth often comes from trusting the unknown. Over two decades, she built a global career in brand marketing and human insights, leading strategy for brands including Nike, Old Spice, Audi, Levi’s, Samsung, and Coca-Cola. Her campaigns have earned more than 15 awards for creativity and effectiveness, including a Cannes Gold Lion and multiple Effies. Sunaina has held senior leadership roles, including VP, Global Head of Insights at HP, where she helped grow brand value by 25% and generate over $500M in new services revenue. She also led strategy at MullenLowe Boston, Wieden+Kennedy Delhi, and Leo Burnett Delhi, guiding initiatives that bridged creativity, technology, and culture. Today, she serves on the board of the Design Forward Alliance, a nonprofit advancing human-centered design as a force for public good and organizing San Diego Design Week, a major cultural and economic event. Her personal journey mirrors the work she now facilitates through Design Your Next — helping women reclaim purpose, creativity, and agency as they navigate complexity and change. Sunaina has made bold personal and professional pivots, navigating setbacks, loss, and disillusionment to live a life of independence and empathy. She completed a Master’s in Strategic Design for Global Leadership at Parsons School of Design at the age of 50, and continues to explore how inspire and activate creative confidence in women. She also holds degrees in Integrated Marketing Communications and Architecture. A certified Japanese saké sommelier, she hosts tasting sessions that explore how sensory experience, creativity, and culture can reconnect us with ourselves and each other — a practice she approaches with the same curiosity and care as her work in design and leadership.

Sunaina Mehta is the Founder of Design Your Next, a transformative program helping ambitious women reclaim their purpose and design success on their own terms. Her work is rooted in a truth she's lived her entire life: choosing yourself is the most graceful act of rebellion.

Born between America and India, Sunaina learned early what it means to fight for your place. At twelve, she was demoted in an Indian school where she didn’t speak the language, then rose to the top of her class. That resilience became her signature.

Over two decades, Sunaina built a powerhouse career in brand strategy and marketing, leading work for Nike, Old Spice, Audi, Levi's, Samsung, Coca-Cola, and HP, where she served as Global Head of Insights, helping grow brand value by 25% and generate over $500M in new services revenue. Her campaigns earned 15+ awards, including a Cannes Gold Lion and multiple Effies. But when corporate success began to cost her integrity and empathy, she walked away.

At 50, she went back to Parsons School of Design, got divorced, sent her son to college, sold her house, and moved to LA. Through dismantling and rebuilding her life, she discovered: transformation comes from reconnecting intellect with intuition.

That insight became Design Your Next, an 8-week program drawing on strategic design thinking, method acting, and brand storytelling to help women define their purpose, hone their voice, and create action plans. A PhD architect now drives a school of thought in architecture. A venture capitalist has reclaimed her courage to finance sustainable systems. Women who once felt lost now lead movements.

She serves on the board of the Design Forward Alliance and is a certified Japanese saké sommelier, exploring how sensory experience reconnects us with creativity and self-expression.

She lives unapologetically on her own terms in Los Angeles, modeling the graceful rebellion she teaches.

column50:
Serin Silva is a strategic advisor to women founder-CEOs and leadership teams running $15–$50M companies who are in the thick of growth, complexity, or outright chaos. She’s the person they call when something big has to change—restructuring a production company for the AI era, turning “accidental” agencies into real businesses, or helping a team go from $10M to $25M in under a year without burning the place down.

Before advising founders, Serin spent two decades inside high-pressure corporate environments where the stakes were very real. She led integration communications for eight acquisitions in a single year at a billion-dollar telecom; helped bring Oracle’s first CRM and Human Capital Management platforms to market; launched the original MSNBC digital brand for Microsoft; and drove strategy for an early food-delivery robot long before it was cool. At a major consumer brand, her digital marketing work generated $500M in revenue in one year across five product lines. She’s led five restructures and turnarounds for mid-sized companies, doubled revenue for a midmarket tech firm, and delivered a 150% business increase in eight months by forcing the right business and mindset pivots.

Born in Silicon Valley to Turkish-Cypriot immigrants who fled civil war, Serin grew up reading rooms, managing volatility, and spotting patterns other people miss. Her approach is simple: set the strategy, align the people and incentives, name the truth, and get moving. She blends sharp strategic frameworks with deep human insight and intuition—what she calls “wild love”: fierce care plus clear calls. Her creative work has been published twice, and she designs retreats and ceremonial spaces for high-performing women who need somewhere honest to fall apart and reassemble.

On stage, Serin is direct, funny, and deeply human. She speaks about leading through inflection points, navigating restructures and turnarounds, building companies in the age of AI without losing the humans, and how women leaders can trust their intuition without sacrificing rigor. If you want polite progress talk, she’s not your person. If you want your audience to feel seen, challenged, and ready to actually do something different on Monday morning, she’s your force multiplier and secret weapon.

column50:
Jeff Wetherhold helps leaders master the conversations that actually create change. One skeptical teammate, one cautious department, and one eye roll at a time. Plans don't change people. Conversations do.

As Founder of MI for Health, Jeff has set leaders up to succeed at change for more than 20 years. Traditional change management fails 88% of the time, and usually in the exact same way. It focuses on strategy while neglecting the biggest challenge leaders face: engaging and motivating people who are uncertain about change. 

That’s where Jeff’s work begins. His approach draws from motivational interviewing, behavioral science, and evidence-driven communication skills to help leaders co-create change with skeptical audiences. No jargon. No vague platitudes. Skills you can start using today.

Jeff has advised organizations like the State of Illinois, the Institute for Healthcare Improvement, MIT, and the University of Nebraska Medical Center. He's trained thousands of professionals in how to surface what's unsaid, shift difficult conversations, and build shared momentum instead of forced compliance. Known for his clarity, calm, and dry humor, Jeff brings grounded presence to complex environments where the stakes are high and the skepticism is higher.

A former behavioral science researcher, Jeff holds a Master's in Education from Harvard University and is faculty with the Institute for Healthcare Improvement. He's also a Prosci Certified Change Practitioner and member of the Motivational Interviewing Network of Trainers.

Jeff lives in Maine with his family, where he hikes, visits the ocean as often as possible, eats more locally made donuts than is probably wise, and stays deeply curious about how people grow—individually and collectively.

column50:
Nick Montalbine is founder of Inner Voice Analytics, the consultancy leaders call when something feels off, but the data says everything’s fine. His team helps organizations catch the truth beneath the surface, using continuous listening and behavioral analytics to spot cultural cracks before they spread.

Through his work, Nick helps leaders tune in to what’s really happening beneath the surface, catching disengagement early, and shifting culture before it breaks. His method combines behavioral science and AI-driven listening to build workplaces where people feel seen, heard, and energized to stay.  His work doesn’t just reduce attrition, it increases the odds of successful change.

He’s spent over 20 years driving transformation inside Fortune 100s, deploying strategy, scaling culture, and leading ops across Nike, healthcare, finance, and retail. (Yes, he’s a Master Black Belt in Lean Six Sigma. Yes, that’s a real thing.)

At Nike, he led HR strategy for global retail and digital teams, supporting 70,000+ employees. At Legacy Health, he leveraged continuous employee listening to guide large-scale organizational change, surfacing hidden risks early and boosting adoption across two major hospital systems. In one effort, his work engaged over 300 clinicians and uncovered cultural cracks that traditional approaches were missing entirely.

Nick’s the person leaders call when performance is dipping, trust is fraying, or culture’s quietly cracking beneath the surface. Whether it’s early warning signs or full-blown cultural combustion, he helps leaders steady the ship and rebuild what matters.

When he’s not tracking the pulse of workplace culture, Nick’s in Oregon’s Willamette Valley with his wife and daughter, where they grow grapes and build a life as intentional as the workplaces he helps shape.

column49: Stephen is a former educator, now leveraging his unique strengths to influence the impact space—including philanthropy, impact investing, and nonprofits—toward a more harmonious existence. He focuses on fostering true collaboration and partnership while disrupting the harmful power dynamics perpetuating inefficiencies and obstacles. Much of what he brings to the table was shaped by his years as a high school PE teacher, Athletic Director, and basketball coach in Watts, and he brings that same energy and passion to every role he takes on. Stephen joined UpMetrics in 2015 after recognizing a unique opportunity to uplift communities. As Vice President of Community, he empowers partners to leverage information and relationships to accelerate progress toward their goals—whether through making more informed decisions, gaining new insights, or amplifying their stories. His passion lies in building capacity for impact organizations, and he has dedicated his life to advancing youth and communities through education and development. Throughout his career, Stephen has served Southern California’s young people in multiple capacities, including as a teacher, coach, athletic director, district coordinator for after-school programs, and adjunct professor. In 2015, he was honored as the CIF Los Angeles City Section Athletic Director of the Year. Today, he continues his community involvement as a board member with A Reason To Survive and as an inaugural board member and board chair for MENTOR California. A firm believer in the power of perpetual positivity, Stephen is committed to lending his time, talent, and resources to help others succeed, believing that’s what the world needs more of. He holds a BS in Physical Education and an MA in Secondary Education from Pepperdine and lives in San Diego with his wife and three daughters.
column50:
Stephen Minix is a former educator turned impact strategist who’s disrupting the power dynamics that hold the social sector back. As Vice President of Community at UpMetrics, he sits at the intersection of philanthropy, nonprofits, and data—helping mission-driven orgs cut through noise, deepen their storytelling, and build coalitions that actually work.

Stephen’s approach is anything but business-as-usual. Raised in the Pacific Northwest and professionally forged in Watts as a high school PE teacher, basketball coach, and Athletic Director, he brings a rare combination of street smarts, soul, and systems thinking. His throughline? Creating spaces where equity isn’t just discussed—it’s designed into the fabric of how people gather, decide, and lead.

Over the years, he’s earned his stripes: from being honored as CIF LA City Section’s Athletic Director of the Year, to mentoring youth, to advising boards like MENTOR California and A Reason To Survive. Whether he’s convening national funders in New Orleans or amplifying community voices on a podcast mic, Stephen leads with positivity, grit, and a deep belief in collective impact. He doesn’t just talk about change; he catalyzes it.

Stephen holds degrees from Pepperdine and lives in San Diego with his wife and three daughters. He’s here to tell the truth, elevate the overlooked, and inspire action—one story at a time.

Tim Beattie is the CEO and co-founder of Stellafai - the strategy execution toolkit for goal-oriented teams who want to do more than just track tasks. Stellafai provides coaching, connections and insights to level up organisations' outcome games.

What drives Tim is motivating others through energy, personality and fun. 

Bigger, wider, stronger seeds of inspiration that stand a better chance of growing further.

column50:
Tim Beattie is the co-founder of Stellafai, an outcome-coaching platform and operating system to connect product, sales and customer success teams. 

Tim advocates for changing traditional consulting models, which he believes are broken. Instead, he envisions a shift towards enabling services, where asynchronous coaching empowers teams to become self-sufficient and continually improve.

He has over 20 years of experience driving transformation at global organizations, having consulted at PwC, IBM, Deloitte, and BAE Systems before serving as Global Head of Product at Red Hat Open Innovation Labs.

Under his leadership, Stellafai has disrupted the consulting model, enabled boutique consultancies to scale, and helped technical teams in the cloud infrastructure space articulate and accelerate their impact through measurable outcomes.

Tim is the co-author of the best-selling book DevOps Culture and Practice and a sought-after speaker who regularly shares his insights on why transformation initiatives stall and how being outcome-focused can unlock lasting change through keynotes, podcasts, and thought leadership platforms. 

When he’s not driving outcomes at Stellafai, Tim is either with his family and black Labrador, Gerrard, or chasing miles in marathon training, proving that he never stops running toward progress, on the track or in business.

column49:
Dan Balcauski is the founder and Chief Pricing Officer at Product Tranquility, where he focuses on helping high-volume B2B SaaS CEOs define pricing and packaging for new products. He is a TopTal certified Top 3% Product Management Professional and helps teach Kellogg Executive Education course on Product Strategy.

Over the last 15 years, Dan has managed multiple products throughout the product life-cycle from new concept incubation, product launch, product maintenance, platform transitions, and end of life. Dan has worked in both consumer and B2B companies across consumer internet, mobile, IT software, and test and measurement hardware and software and company sizes ranging from startups to publicly traded multi-national enterprises.

Before founding Product Tranquility, Dan completed a solo round-the-world expedition to 21 countries, a lifelong dream that involved several life-changing volunteering experiences and a new collection of friends and good stories. Before that, he was Head of Product at LawnStarter, a two-sided marketplace bringing the best lawn care professionals to customers across the US. Dan joined LawnStarter after SolarWinds’ $4B acquisition by Silver Lake and Thoma Bravo. At SolarWinds, he was a Principal Product Strategist where he helped form and execute product growth strategies across new and existing products, both on-premises and SaaS-based.

Dan has a Bachelor of Science in Computer Engineering from Iowa State University and an MBA from the Kellogg School of Management at Northwestern University.

column49:
Yosi Amram, Ph.D., is a licensed clinical psychologist, a CEO leadership coach, a pioneering researcher in the field of spiritual intelligence, and a best-selling award-winning author. Previously the founder and CEO of two companies that he has led through successful IPOs, Yosi has coached over 100 CEOs many of whom have built companies with thousands of employees and revenues in the billions.  

With engineering degrees from MIT, an MBA from Harvard, and a Ph.D. in Psychology from Sofia University, he is a pioneering researcher in the field of spiritual intelligence, whose research has received over 1000 citations.  As the author of the Amazon Best-Seller and Nautilus Book Award Gold Medal-winning Spiritually Intelligent Leadership: How to Inspire by Being Inspired, Yosi is committed awakening greater spiritual intelligence in himself and the world. 

Spiritual Intelligence (SI) is not a belief in higher power or God, nor is it an experience of awakening, or a momentary realization of Oneness. Rather, SI is deepest and highest expression of our life force energy and sacred spark of life involving to the ability to draw on, embody, and express qualities and virtues hailed by all the world's spiritual traditions, such as purpose, service, trust, gratitude, compassion, presence, humility, integrity, and higher-self, to name just a few.  These are the qualities that naturally arise when connecting to and rooting ourselves in our spirit essence, whereby we feel inner-, inter-, and ultra-connected, connected to ourselves, connected to others, and connected to the sacred and transcendent dimension of life. 

Yosi began his leadership journey in the Israeli military where he received numerous awards and garnered the fastest promotion record in his regiment’s history. Despite these accomplishments, the military chafed at his soul, eventually inspiring Yosi’s lifelong quest for more humane approaches to leadership, which led him to start and build his own companies.

Yosi is also the founder of several non-profits, including trueMASCULINITY.org, Engendering-Love.org, and AwakeningSI.org. 
For further information about Yosi and his work, please see www.yosiamram.net and/or subscribe to his YouTube channel @AwakeningSpirituaIntelligence.  To learn more about spiritual intelligence and to receive your free assessment of your spiritual intelligence profile, or to assess your spiritually intelligent leadership competencies, including self- and 360-assessments, visit www.intelligensi.com.

column49: Referred to as the “guru of the DTC movement,” by Inc. Magazine and the “man who kickstarted the direct brand economy,” by IAB, David is co-founder and President at Idea Farm Ventures (IFV), a consumer and retail investment company based in New York. David was an early investor in iconic new economy brands including Diapers.com (acq. by Amazon for $545m), Jet.com (acq. by Walmart for $3.3b), Bonobos (acq. by Walmart for $310m), Burrow (private) Cotopaxi, (private), Dagne Dover (private), Harry’s (private), and Warby Parker (WRBY), among many others. In July 2025, Idea Farm portfolio company Touchland was acquired by Church & Dwight for $880M in a landmark transaction in the consumer space. Brightland, Caraway, Glowbar, Lemon Perfect, Minoan, and Petfolk are among the standouts in the portfolio. Before IFV, David had a 20-year academic career at Wharton where he was a chaired professor, award-winning teacher and researcher, and originator of courses on digital marketing and e-commerce. His research awards from the Institute for Operations Research and the Management Sciences (INFORMS) include the Frank M. Bass Outstanding Dissertation Award, John D.C. Little Best Paper Finalist Award (three), Marketing Science Long Term Impact Finalist Award (two), and Best Operations Management Paper in Management Science Finalist Award (two). In addition, he received the First Place Award for the Production and Operations Management Society Applied Research Challenge, Workshop in Information Systems and Economics Overall Best Paper Award, among several others. David speaks globally at events for clients including Alibaba, Google, Haier, Mastercard, Microsoft, Nike, and Ralph Lauren as well for founders and entrepreneurs. His first book, Location is (Still) Everything: The Surprising Influence of the Real World on How We Search, Shop, and Sell in the Virtual One, provides a playbook for how to win in the digital economy. Two recent MIT Sloan Management articles “How to Win in an Omnichannel World” and “The Store is Dead—Long Live the Store” are bestsellers at Harvard Business School Publishing. He has published peer-reviewed articles in all premier academic journals in the marketing field, i.e., Journal of Consumer Research, Journal of Marketing, Journal of Marketing Research, Management Science, and Marketing Science. David holds a Ph.D. (Business) and M.S. (Statistics) from Stanford University and an MA from University of Pennsylvania as well as a B. Com and M. Com (1st Class Honors) from University of Auckland.
